title: Starter Momentum Total-Return Guard
hypothesis: A starter-universe momentum branch running on EODHD-managed reference and split-and-dividend total-return inputs can test whether recent momentum failures were partly data-contract failures rather than only strategy-shape failures.
why_different_from_retired: The retired momentum branches changed ranking, basket width, and portfolio controls, but they all relied on the earlier vendor-adjusted daily-bar path with static/sample support files.
This branch changes the evidence contract itself by using generated EODHD exchange metadata plus split-and-dividend action feeds to build the canonical dataset before the governed momentum seed runs.
success_criteria: The total-return starter seed must pass the existing validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion policy before it can justify promotion or a broader total-return follow-up family.
If it fails, it must still show whether the managed input path changed evidence quality enough to keep total-return-aware momentum in the governed backlog.

stop_conditions.when: The total-return starter seed passes validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion rules under the current policy.
The seed records a terminal promotion decision and still fails the policy, especially if the cleaner data path still cannot deliver benchmark-relative split or walk-forward evidence.

bootstrap.objective: Run the starter-universe momentum baseline on EODHD-managed total-return inputs so the governed queue can measure whether cleaner corporate-action handling changes the evidence shape enough to justify a broader follow-up.
bootstrap.branch_rationale: The retired momentum branches relied on vendor-adjusted bars plus static/sample support files. This branch changes the input contract itself by using generated EODHD reference data and split-and-dividend total-return reconstruction before deciding whether momentum should spend more governed backlog depth.
positive_hypotheses: A managed vendor-backed instrument master and action ledger can remove data-quality distortions that were contaminating benchmark-relative evidence.
Reconstructed total-return inputs can improve split and walk-forward stability enough to justify a later broad-universe follow-up if the starter-universe signal shape improves.

seed_stack.label: Current starter momentum baseline
seed_stack.rationale: Control branch using the existing vendor-adjusted EODHD path.

seed_stack.label: Managed total-return starter seed
seed_stack.rationale: Same strategy family, but running on EODHD-managed reference and corporate-action inputs.

campaign_path.label: Momentum Total-Return Starter Seed
campaign_path.purpose: Execute the managed total-return starter branch and decide whether the improved data path earns broader governed follow-up.
